title: Will you be sad when it all ends
display_title: Will you be sad when it all ends
wikitext:
[]: Will you be sad when it all ends?             Quote Post by CCri » Sat Apr 20, 2013 9:46 pm After 4.0 is released, it is officially all over. No more hope for anything new, nothing. the end. For me, its story that i have been following for over 13 yrs. Even though its just an anime, its still something that i've thought about or i picked up every so often for half my life. so i cant help but have grown an attachment to the franchise and the characters over the year. I dont know about you but im going to be pretty sad when its all over

[]: Re: Will you be sad when it all ends?             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Apr 20, 2013 9:54 pm Are you seriously believing that it's going to "end"? Where is it written?

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sat Apr 20, 2013 9:56 pm As far as I am concerned it ended in 1997 with the End of Evangelion. It ended before I was born.

[]: Re: Will you be sad when it all ends?             Quote Post by CCri » Sat Apr 20, 2013 10:33 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Are you seriously believing that it's going to "end"? Where is it written?

[]: Re: Will you be sad when it all ends?             Quote Post by Jadley » Sat Apr 20, 2013 10:45 pm CCri wrote: i thought it was supposed to end after 4.0. unless you think they are going to make a tv series reboot lol.

[]: Re: Will you be sad when it all ends?             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Apr 20, 2013 10:45 pm CCri wrote: i thought it was supposed to end after 4.0. unless you think they are going to make a tv series reboot lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Apr 21, 2013 12:08 am Lavinius wrote: As far as I am concerned it ended in 1997 with the End of Evangelion. It ended before I was born.

[]:             Quote Post by CCri » Sun Apr 21, 2013 1:36 am i dont understand why they cant just extend the movie series. they seem to be generating a lot of profit for it and a lot of people dont mind paying to watch more of it. if anything theyre trying so hard to cram so much material into 4 movies since they have material for waaay more.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 21, 2013 1:46 am Evangelion occupies a place in Japanese popular culture not dissimilar to that of Star Trek in the US. Asuka, Rei, Shinko, Tabuko and now Mari will continue to be whored out for commercial endorsements and pachinko games for a while to come, even if there are no new manga, game or anime products. And if Sadamoto does a Rebuild manga, we'd be guaranteed nearly 20 years of more stuff and "how will he handle the ending?" discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Apr 21, 2013 2:35 am Yeah, but this is most likely the final time that Anno is gonna lead an Evangelion project. In a sense, that's pretty much the end of the line as far as I'm concerned. Sure, there's gonna be more content/tv series/films/etc--Khara and Gainax would be financially retarded to not make them--but Anno was, for whatever it's worth, the spark behind the whole franchise. It's not gonna feel quite the same without him. I hope that he moves on with something else as swiftly as possible after the series ends......but I get the feeling there's gonna be a 5 year hiatus at least before anything happens.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun Apr 21, 2013 3:28 am No. I was actually satisfied quite well by the EoTV and then the EoE endings. This si coming from someone who does get slightly depressed at the end of most shows (provided they are good).

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sun Apr 21, 2013 4:56 am Yeah probably but only untill we learn about something new.Personally i would like a reboot,with new characters and EVA Units but with a similar setting like the post second impact and Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 21, 2013 8:33 am Really, the show had ended by the time I first got to it, so 4.0 coming out will just be a return to the status quo. No big deal.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Sun Apr 21, 2013 9:14 am I'm sure they'll make more... "Evangelion 6.66" is too good to pass up.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Apr 21, 2013 9:27 am Obviously the story of NGE won't truly be done until we get a Jet Alone spinoff.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Apr 21, 2013 9:48 am When I got into Eva there had been no word of Rebuild - so really, the Eva was already "over" at that point for me. That hasn't really changed. The franchise itself of course will continue from hereon too with Rebuild or not but the original story was finished long ago

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Apr 21, 2013 10:49 am It won't end after Final/4.0, just the Rebuild world has far too much material so Gainax and Khara won't take advantage of it. And I refuse to imagine that it could end as long as we don't have a spin-off of Evangelion 3.0  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Pleb » Sun Apr 21, 2013 11:05 am I signed up just for this thread because I felt the need to. Evangelion will never end.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Apr 21, 2013 11:19 am Nah, I don't think Evangelion is going anywhere any time soon. I was trolling through some of the old sections of the forum from around 2004, and I found some quotes that said things like "Evangelion is never going to get a sequel", and "Evangelion is over". We're in that stage right now, pondering what will happen with FINAL is over and done with. We'll be there even more so after FINAL has actually been released. Do I thing we're going to get a break from Eva for a while? Yes. Do I think that it's going to be gone forever? Not one bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sun Apr 21, 2013 1:21 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Do I thing we're going to get a break from Eva for a while? Yes. Do I think that it's going to be gone forever? Not one bit.
summary: []: Will you be sad when it all ends?             Quote Post by CCri » Sat Apr 20, 2013 9:46 pm After 4.0 is released, it is officially all over. No more hope for anything new, nothing. the end. For me, its story that i have been following for over 13 yrs. Even though its just an anime, its still something that i've thought about or i picked up every so often for half my life. so i cant help but have grown an attachment to the franchise
